description: REQ-FEED-023 acceptance criterion (behavioral-spec.md line 529) explicitly requires 'EditorPane is wrapped in a <main> with class editor-main'. The fix for FIND-S2-02 correctly resolved the HTML5 double-<main> violation by changing +page.svelte line 104 from '<main class="editor-main">' to '<div class="editor-main">'. However, the behavioral spec was never amended to reflect this change. The spec remains authoritative per VCSDD process: it still mandates a <main> element. The implementation now satisfies HTML5 but contradicts the spec's stated acceptance criterion. Because this arose from a necessary architectural correction (AppShell already provides the document-level <main>), the spec must be updated to align with the implementation — but that update has not been made. The test in main-route.dom.vitest.ts line 202-210 verifies that the pattern '<main class="editor-main">' does NOT exist (testing the fix, not the spec criterion), so the test now contradicts the spec's acceptance criterion rather than validating it.
crossReference: .vcsdd/features/ui-feed-list-actions/specs/behavioral-spec.md:529 — 'EditorPane is wrapped in a <main> with class editor-main'
promptnotes/src/routes/__tests__/main-route.dom.vitest.ts:202-210 — test asserts <main class="editor-main"> does NOT exist, contradicting spec acceptance criterion
resolution: Update REQ-FEED-023 acceptance criterion in behavioral-spec.md to replace '<main>' with '<div>' in the EditorPane wrapper requirement. The AppShell already provides the document-level <main>; the inner content wrapper should be <div>. Alternatively, if semantic HTML requirement is preserved, AppShell.svelte must change its <main> to <div> and +page.svelte must restore <main class='editor-main'>.
